title: Nezumi Steals Nami’s Hope
scene: On pages 5–8, the story moves through Arlong Park and Nami’s dangerous position among the fish-men. A Marine force confronts Arlong, but the scene exposes how terrifying and entrenched his pirates are.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The hell do you think you're doing!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What do you mean? We've obviously come to get Nami back.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Now's not the time to be wasting time!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That idiot got himself caught by Arlong! If we don't hurry up, he'll be k...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: was Mur- Dered by sister Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Look, the shore's been all gouged out.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: But there're no traces of anything here. Not even the smell of gunpowder.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Just what on earth happened here...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It doesn't look like any marine ship I've ever seen.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It must not be from the Marine Base near this region.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Can you see the Marine Ship!?

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: Yeah! The government sent it to aid Gosa Village!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They've sent a ship for us!

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: That's right! Some of the survivors from my village managed to contact the government!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: So is that the rumoured Arlong Park?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: The world must be coming to an end if pirates can set up a cozy base and strut their stuff like that.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong Pirates
dialogue.text: Is only to rescue the survivors of Gosa Village which was recently destroyed by the ARLong Pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Does that allow us, who stand for justice,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: To simply overlook a pirate fortress right in front of our eyes!?

summary: This part builds the Arlong Park conflict by showing how Nami’s choices look contradictory from outside while Arlong’s power keeps the island trapped.
